content.problemStatement:
Chef is given a tree with NNN nodes and a positive integer KKK. Each node is assigned a value AiA_iAi​.

Chef has to divide the tree into a forest of exactly KKK trees, numbered from 111 to KKK, such that each node belongs to **exactly one** tree.

Let BiB_iBi​ denote the bitwise XOR of the values of all nodes belonging to the ithi^{th}ith tree.

Find the **maximum** possible value of B1B_1B1​ &\&& B2B_2B2​ &…\& \dots&… &\&& BKB_KBK​, where &\&& denotes the bitwise AND operation.


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT, the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines:

The first line contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK — the number of nodes in the tree and a positive integer as mentioned in the statement, respectively.
The next line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1,A_2,\ldots,A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​, the values assigned to each node.
The next (N−1)(N-1)(N−1) lines describe the edges. The ithi^{th}ith of these (N−1)(N-1)(N−1) lines contains two space-separated integers uiu_iui​ and viv_ivi​, denoting an edge between nodes uiu_iui​ and viv_ivi​.




Output Format
For each test case, print a single integer, the **maximum** possible value of B1B_1B1​ &\&& B2B_2B2​ &…\& \dots&… &\&& BKB_KBK​
constraints: Sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: A possible way of dividing the nodes into 222 trees is [1,3,6][1,3,6][1,3,6] and [2,4,5][2,4,5][2,4,5].

B1=A1⊕A3⊕A6=1⊕2⊕5=6B_1 = A_1\oplus A_3\oplus A_6 = 1\oplus 2\oplus 5 = 6B1​=A1​⊕A3​⊕A6​=1⊕2⊕5=6
B2=A2⊕A4⊕A5=4⊕2⊕1=7B_2 = A_2\oplus A_4\oplus A_5 = 4\oplus 2\oplus 1 = 7B2​=A2​⊕A4​⊕A5​=4⊕2⊕1=7

Thus, B1B_1B1​ &\&& B2=6B_2 = 6B2​=6 &\&& 7=67 = 67=6.
Note that this is the maximum possible value of B1B_1B1​ &\&& B2B_2B2​ you can obtain.
Another way of dividing is [1,2,4,5][1,2,4,5][1,2,4,5] and [3,6][3,6][3,6], which leads to the same answer.
Test case 222: A possible way of dividing the nodes into 333 trees is [1,3,6,7,8][1,3,6,7,8][1,3,6,7,8], [4][4][4], and [2,5][2,5][2,5]

B1=A1⊕A3⊕A6⊕A7⊕A8=1⊕5⊕9⊕0⊕8=5B_1 = A_1\oplus A_3\oplus A_6\oplus A_7\oplus A_8 = 1\oplus 5\oplus 9 \oplus 0\oplus 8 = 5B1​=A1​⊕A3​⊕A6​⊕A7​⊕A8​=1⊕5⊕9⊕0⊕8=5
B2=A4=14B_2 = A_4 = 14B2​=A4​=14
B3=A2⊕A5=13⊕2=15B_3 = A_2\oplus A_5 = 13\oplus 2 = 15B3​=A2​⊕A5​=13⊕2=15.

Thus, B1B_1B1​ &\&& B2B_2B2​ &\&& B3=5B_3 = 5B3​=5 &\&& 141414 &\&& &\&& 15=415 = 415=4.
Note that this is the maximum possible value of B1B_1B1​ &\&& B2B_2B2​ &\&& B3B_3B3​ you can obtain.
